top_categories.workout_context: 180 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Trek Across Maine's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
Trek Across Maine's rolling paved hills of central Maine forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 180 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 180 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
180 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Trek Across Maine's rolling paved hills of central Maine rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
At Trek Across Maine, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 8,000ft of climbing on rolling paved hills of central Maine does to your power file on race day.
8,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Trek Across Maine. Train it until it feels boring.
8,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Trek Across Maine, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: June in New Gloucester, Maine, USA can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Trek Across Maine. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 180 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 180 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Trek Across Maine requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling paved hills of central maine to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 180 miles of rolling paved hills of central maine in New Gloucester, Maine, USA.
